title: High Hauler
content:
{{Top|leg|italics=1}}
'''''High Hauler''''' was an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[transport/Legends|transport]] that was captured by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] in [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]] and [[Mission to Ruusan|used to sneak]] a Rebel strike team onto the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]] through [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec's]] blockade. 

==Description==
''High Hauler'' normally contained a crew of twenty-five, but a crew as small as twelve could operate the vessel. The ship had a docking bay that could accommodate two smaller transports at once, such as [[light freighter/Legends|light freighters]] and [[shuttle/Legends|shuttles]].<ref name="JK" />

==History==
The ''High Hauler'' was an [[Imperial Remnant|Imperial]] [[transport/Legends|transport]] that was part of the [[task force/Legends|task force]] of an [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Imperial Star Destroyer]]. The force was involved in the [[battle in the Milagro system]] which resulted to the [[Crynyd|Star Destroyer's]] surrender and capture by the Rebel forces stationed around [[Milagro/Legends|Milagro]].<ref name="JK">''[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight]]''</ref><ref name="JKaudio">[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Jedi Knight'' audio drama]]</ref>

===New Republic service===
The battle coincided with the [[search for the Valley of the Jedi]], where Rebel [[agent/Legends|agent]] and fledgling [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Kyle Katarn]] was after the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]] who invaded the lost planet [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]], a location of a powerful [[Vergence/Legends|Force nexus]]. [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]] initially didn't condone Katarn's quest, mentioning, among others, Jerec's [[Vengeance Battle Group|blockade]] around the planet; his partner, [[Jan Ors]], backed by [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], suggested to use a captured transport, available after the recent battle. Solo volunteered to pilot it but he was dissuaded by [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], and Ors recruited [[prison/Legends|prisoner]] [[Alfonso Luiz Obota]] for the task.<ref name="JK" /><ref name="JKaudio" />

''High Hauler''{{'s}} cover story was that it was taking a shipment of 250 [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedoes]] from the [[Corporate Sector/Legends|Corporate Sector]] to [[Byss/Legends|Byss]] when it came under Rebel attack. Most of the bridge crew, including [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Drax]], were killed and the engineering officer [[Alfonso Luiz Obota|Hortu Agar]] had been forced to assume command.<ref name="JK" /> Dropping out of hypespace, the closest system was [[Ruusan system/Legends|Hoth's Brand system]].<ref name="JKaudio" /> An old [[HWK-290_light_freighter/Legends|HWK-290 light freighter]] was in the bay, replacing a lost [[shuttle/Legends|shuttle]].<ref name="JK" /><ref name="JKaudio" /> The ''High Hauler''{{'s}} interior had dismantled cables, hull patches and blackened surfaces, either from the earlier battle, or pursposedfully done so by the Rebel agents, who also wore [[Imperial officer's uniform/Legends|Imperial uniforms]], blood-stained bandages and makeup in order to look wounded Imperials.<ref name="JK" />

All twelve members of the strike team were male humans, reflecting the demographics of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]], except Ors who remained hidden. Obota was to impersonate Agar while Katarn posed as an Imperial [[Warrant Officer/Legends|Warrant Officer]].<ref name="JK" />

===Mission to Ruusan===
Upon her arrival to [[Ruusan system/Legends|Hoth's Brand system]], she was approached by an [[EF76_Nebulon-B_escort_frigate/Legends|escort frigate]] and Imperial fighters and Jerec's flagship, the [[Vengeance (Vengeance-class)|''Vengeance'']], requested confirmation codes. "Agar" mentioned that as an [[engineer/Legends|engineer]] replacing a captain, did not know the codes, and mentioned their shipment of torpedoes, picking the [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officer]]'s interest, confirming Ors's suggestion that crucial supplies would make the Imperials forget all about verifications and formalities.<ref name="JK" />

[[File:Youhavebeentricked.jpg|thumb|Obota with the Imperial Lieutenant at the docking bay.]]
Soon an [[assault shuttle/Legends|assault shuttle]] docked to the bay; in the following fourty-five [[standard minute/Legends|minutes]] a [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] with [[Stormtrooper/Legends|Stormtroopers]] inspected the [[Bridge_(starship)/Legends|bridge]], the [[engine/Legends|engineering]] and the hull damage. Back to the bay, [[Unidentified Imperial lieutenant (Ruusan)|the Imperial]] inquired about the [[HWK-290_light_freighter/Legends|HWK-290 light freighter]] docked there, where Ors was hiding, spying on their communications. Satisfied with "Agar"'s explanations, the Lieutentant was ready to inspect the torpedoes before trans-shipping, but this boring task was skipped when the "warrant officer" alerted that a [[lighter]] was ready to dock for transshipment. Twelve load lifter droids loaded the sabotaged torpedoes to the lighter, a process that took three trips.<ref name="JK" />

The ''High Hauler'' stayed in the system for repairs for the next dozen hours, while the ''[[Moldy Crow/Legends|Moldy Crow]]'' became part of the [[battle group/Legends|battle group]]; having clearance, it left for hyperspace, but not before passing through a [[Class I security zone]] and the upper [[atmosphere/Legends|stratosphere]] of Ruusan, where the ''Crow'' secretly launched from its bay.<ref name="JK" />

==Appearances==
*''[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight]]''
*[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Jedi Knight'' audio drama]]
